content:
from Week 5 Discussion
Feb 4, 2025 4:14PM
Lauren Akke
Hey Jenn, Your response does a great job of highlighting the significance of symbolism in A Worn Path, particularly in how Phoenix’s name and journey represent perseverance and sacrifice. I appreciate your analysis of the phoenix as a mythical creature that endures hardships and rises again, much like Phoenix Jackson herself. Her ability to push forward despite physical and emotional challenges speaks volumes about her strength and devotion to her grandson.

Building on your analysis, another interesting layer to Phoenix’s journey is how the obstacles she faces symbolize societal and racial hardships. As an elderly Black woman navigating a world filled with both physical barriers and subtle discrimination, her perseverance takes on a broader meaning. For example, when the hunter she encounters lifts his gun toward her, she remains unfazed, saying, "Doesn’t the gun scare you?" and she simply replies, "No, sir, I seen plenty go off closer by, in my day, and for less than what I done." This moment not only shows her resilience but also alludes to the struggles she has likely endured in her lifetime, reinforcing the theme of perseverance beyond just the physical journey.
